content:
# TensorRT FP16 for RAUNE-Net — Final Decision Record (2026-04-12)

## What: Manual ONNX→TRT FP16 engine for RAUNE-Net inference, opt-in via --tensorrt flag

## Key Design Decisions:
1. Manual ONNX→TRT over torch_tensorrt (Python 3.13 blocker + InstanceNorm graph fragmentation)
2. FP16 only, no INT8 (INT8's 256 levels insufficient for 10-bit pipeline's 1024 levels)
3. PREFER_PRECISION_CONSTRAINTS builder flag ensures InstanceNorm variance stays in FP32
4. CUDA event-based inter-stream synchronization instead of full stream sync (avoids CPU stall)
5. Atomic engine cache writes via tempfile+rename to prevent corruption on crash
6. torch.compile gated behind separate --torch-compile flag (not bundled with TRT)

## Performance Results (4K footage, RTX 3060):
- TRT FP16: 6.3 fps (128ms/frame GPU) vs PyTorch fp16: 4.5 fps (191ms/frame GPU) = 1.5x speedup
- Quality: 51.3 dB PSNR avg, all frames > 40dB (spec threshold)
- Engine build: ~3 min first run, <2s cached

## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- TRT execute_async_v3 on a private stream races with default stream — use CUDA events
- TRT engine I/O bindings may be FP32 even with FP16 flag — engine casts internally
- RAUNE-Net trims 2 pixels in height due to conv padding — handle shape mismatch downstream
- Pre-allocate output buffers to avoid cudaMalloc in hot inference loop
- CUDA stream race condition caused black frames — only manifests under GPU load, not isolated tests
